82

The Things We Miss

Serenacula

Summary:

In which Heather discovers Praem is not quite as mature as she'd assumed.

Work Text:

It was quite late, when Heather had the thought. It was an odd idea, one that only occurred to her when she watched Praem and Tenny play a furious game of chess that went far over her head.

"Praem, how old are you?"

"One." Praem immediately responded without looking up from the board.

Heather hesitated. "No, I mean—if you were to describe yourself in human years? I know the concept doesn't fully apply, but..."

She trailed off as Praem looked up to stare at her. Heather almost thought she wasn't going to answer, when she finally spoke:

"Ten," she said, pointing at herself.

Heather blanked at that response.

"Eight," Praem continued, pointing at Tenny.

"Praeeeeem! We're older than that. Fourteen-sixteen!" Teeny trilled.

Praem hesitated for a moment, before pointing to herself again. "Twelve,"

Then pointing at Tenny, "Ten."

"Still older-older," Tenny whined, but Praem seemed settled on those ages and didn't adjust again.

Heather was trying very hard not to panic. Praem was a child? That wasn't right, was it? Had Praem always been a child?

Had she spoken of anything unsuitable in front of her? Heather had assumed she was an adult! In fact she'd been working entirely under that assumption!

"Excuse me a moment," she said weakly, standing up to leave.

Praem's gaze followed her to the door, where Heather closed it behind her.

Then she speed-walked to Evelyn's room, where she found her and Raine. The two had been spending more time together as of late, Evelyn sharing some of the newer anime she'd watched, since apparently Raine needed to catch up on them.

Heather found the two of them cuddling rather more intimately than they typically let her see, when she burst into the room. She did not, in this moment, care. Instead she hastily shut the door, to keep her voice from travelling.

"Heather?" Raine said worriedly, alarm in her expression as she sat up.

"Did you know Praem was a child!?" Heather all but shouted.

Two absolutely baffled faces stared at her.

"I just asked how old she considered herself. She said ten!" Heather was trying hard not to panic at this point.

Evelyn nodded slowly, as though not quite understanding the current conversation. "Yes, I think Praem slightly over-estimates humans, she doesn't fully realise how slowly we learn. She's probably closer to sixteen I'd say..."

Heather stared at her. "Evee, I thought she was an adult!" she hissed.

"What?" Raine said with an involuntary laugh. Both she and Evee looked a bit taken aback, looking at Heather incredulously.

"Heather," Evelyn said slowly, "you were there when we birthed Praem. It was only a year ago. How could she possibly be an adult?"

"But—but she's a demon—"

"Honestly Heather, I know I said they age differently, but she still needs a bit of time to reach that stage," Evelyn said, her voice sharp. "You really thought she was an adult? Did you think Tenny was too? Gosh have you even been paying attention to anything—"

"No, no—I, I knew Tenny was a child," Heather stuttered. "But everyone treats her as a child!"

Evelyn's entire body tensed, her expression souring. "You think I wouldn't have treated Praem like that too, if I'd known!? If I hadn't been too stupid to notice—" she shouted, before curling up as self-recrimination pooled in her expression.

"I didn't notice, and treated her terribly during her childhood, Heather. I basically missed it because I wasn't paying attention. Is that what you want to hear?" Evelyn muttered, heavy guilt creeping into her voice.

Heather was making a mess of this, she realised. "I—"

"You really thought Praem was an adult?" Raine said, no longer smiling as she looked at Heather.

Heather winced, but nodded.

"I would have mentioned it to you if I'd noticed," Raine said, looking a bit lost that she'd missed something that big. "But you treat her like a child. You haven't tried to date her, you don't... swear in front of... oh."

"I had no idea at all," Heather agreed anxiously, searching her thoughts once again for times that she'd treated Praem inappropriately. Talked to her about topics that weren't for children. Far too many examples came to mind.

"Heather, she dresses up as a maid every day because she thinks maids are cool," Raine said gently. "She says whatever silly thing is on her mind. That's not the actions of an adult. I know Evee said sixteen, but in many ways she's much younger than that, maturity-wise. Did you really miss all that?"

"I just thought that was what she liked," Heather said weakly. Though even as she said it, she realised that Praem didn't speak much, always careful with her words. Heather had always accepted it as some sort of Praem-wisdom. Was that also a result of her being young?

"But she takes care of everyone, she does all the chores," Heather added miserably, going to sit on the bed. "She's really doing that as a child?"

"It's what we had her doing during her early months," Evelyn said quietly, her voice pained. "I tried to let her know she didn't have to look after us all, she could have a childhood too like Tenny, even if it's short. But I think she feels more secure knowing she's useful, so I dropped the issue."

"I'm so stupid," Heather said, holding her face in her hands. "She must think I've been favouring Tenny, with how I treat the two of them."

"Why don't you spend a bit of time with her then, Heather?" Raine suggested gently. She was still sitting curled up with Evelyn, an arm carefully stroking her back in comfort. "I had noticed you don't hang out together much."

Heather nodded, standing up. "I'll—I'll do that. We can go out tomorrow and do something together. Aunt-and-niece, or—or mother-and—" she faltered. "I—I probably don't deserve to use that title, huh?"

"Just go spend some time with her, you stupid girl," Evelyn sighed affectionately. "Better late than never."

"Praem, have you ever tried strawberry icecream?" Heather asked gently, when she saw the girl the following morning.

"Supermarket," Praem acknowledged.

"Ever tried it from a proper ice-cream store? It's pretty different."

"No."

"Want to go to the park with me? We could get some together," Heather suggested. It felt a bit awkward, but it was the best she could come up with.

After a pause, Praem stood up and smoothed out her maid uniform with a nod.

"Do you like the ice-cream?" Heather asked, looking at Praem sat next to her on the park bench.

The girl was carefully twisting her ice-cream as she ate, to avoid any of it melting and dripping onto her uniform.

"Yum," Praem agreed in a sing-song tone, Heather picking up just a ghost of happiness in her expressionless face.

Yeah. Yeah, Heather was pretty sure she was an idiot for missing this.
